steps.title: Fastest path: mark the figure first
steps.content: For coordinate-circle questions, read the center and radius from the equation or graph the circle as a check. For diagram facts, mark equal radii, diameters, tangents, and arcs before doing algebra.

steps.title: Recognize the standard form of a circle equation
steps.content: The given equation $(x + 5)^2 + (y + 7)^2 = 144$ matches the standard form $(x - h)^2 + (y - k)^2 = r^2$. In this form, the right side always equals $r^2$, so we can immediately identify that $r^2 = 144$.

steps.title: Solve for the radius
steps.content: Take the square root of both sides to isolate $r$: $$r = \sqrt{144} = 12$$
